environmentBlocker.reason: Mac locked before real VS Code Agent UI discovery and readonly call evidence could be collected

enhancedCapabilities: A stable local stdio MCP adapter exposes an exact 10-tool readonly allowlist backed by shared CLI core services.
Execute-write remains permanently unavailable and readonly policy blocks preview or write surfaces from becoming MCP tools.
JSON-RPC initialize, discovery, calls, parse errors, invalid requests, strict schemas, framing, redaction, and startup behavior have local and independent non-UI evidence.
VS Code Agent is the only distributed client in the 0.5 support denominator; Cursor and Claude Desktop remain explicitly excluded.
unexpectedProblems: VS Code virtualized tool lists made a single viewport appear to expose only 8 of 10 tools during R1.
The first R2 raw-frame harness returned before responses with explicit request IDs arrived, initially misclassifying parse-error and invalid-request behavior.
The Mac locked before R2 could perform the required real VS Code Agent UI verification.
rootCauses: The VS Code Configure Tools UI virtualizes and scrolls the tool list, so one screenshot is not a complete manifest.
The raw JSON-RPC harness keyed its wait on a parsed message object even when intentionally sending malformed raw strings.
The client UI check depended on an interactive authenticated desktop session that was not guaranteed through the end of the validation round.
preventionActions: Require scrolled or searched UI evidence for the complete supported-client tool manifest instead of counting one viewport.
Preflight malformed-frame harnesses against explicit response IDs and preserve supplemental evidence separately from first-attempt scoring.
Before resuming 0.5, preflight an unlocked, authenticated, controllable VS Code Agent session and then create a fresh validator task for the full applicable suite.
remainingRisks: M050-AC-004 is failed because independent real VS Code Agent UI discovery and readonly invocation remain unverified.
The external MCP client runner dependency remains unverified at the 0.5 completion gate.
The 0.50.0 RC candidate must not be described as accepted or released until a fresh validator closes B28/AC004 and all completion gates pass.

nextMilestone.goal: Qualify auditable content operations in an isolated write-test surface: hash-bound preview, explicit approval, minimum-privilege execution, resumability, idempotence, and Agent preview-only behavior.
expectedResults: All 0.6 core acceptance criteria are measured by a fresh novice validator using complete fixed baseline plus separately scored dynamic and adverse suites.
Approved topic and reply workflows produce matching backend/API evidence and real Codex in-app browser visual evidence without residual test resources.
Unsafe, stale, mismatched, duplicate, or unapproved writes are blocked; MCP remains preview-only and exposes no execute-write path.
